text.source_text: स्वशब्दे नै व च सर्वज्ञ ईश्वरो जगतः कारणमिति श्रूयते , श्वेताश्वतराणां मन्त्रोपनिषदि सर्वज्ञमीश्वरं प्रकृत्य — ‘ स कारणं करणाधिपाधिपो न चास्य कश्चिज्जनिता न चाधिपः ’ (श्वे. उ. ६ । ९) इति । तस्मा त्सर्वज्ञं ब्रह्म जगतः कारणम् , नाचेतनं प्रधान मन्यद्वेति सिद्धम् ॥ ११ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: svaśabde nai va ca sarvajña īśvaro jagataḥ kāraṇamiti śrūyate , śvetāśvatarāṇāṃ mantropaniṣadi sarvajñamīśvaraṃ prakṛtya — ‘ sa kāraṇaṃ karaṇādhipādhipo na cāsya kaścijjanitā na cādhipaḥ ’ (śve. u. 6 | 9) iti | tasmā tsarvajñaṃ brahma jagataḥ kāraṇam , nācetanaṃ pradhāna manyadveti siddham || 11 ||

translation.literal_translation: And in a direct word too it is heard that the omniscient Lord alone is cause of the world: in the Svetasvatara mantra-upanishad, after introducing the omniscient Lord, it says, 'He is the cause, the lord of lords of instruments; He has no producer and no overlord.' Therefore it is established that omniscient Brahman is world-cause, not non-conscious Pradhana or anything else. (11)
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.1.11 invokes explicit shruti testimony (svasabda/shrutatva) naming omniscient Ishvara as cause, further excluding Pradhana.
translation.interpretive_note: Direct scriptural naming supplements prior inferential-hermeneutic exclusions.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S01 pass with stable inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit anandamaya technical layering and the long transition bridge in records 1.1.048-1.1.055.
